understood_question: Whether registry lookup caching should be centralized in the registry loader or left to each caller.
take: Centralizing the cache in the loader is cleaner because it gives all callers one freshness policy and one invalidation path.
recommendation: Put a short-TTL cache in the loader, expose an explicit cache-bust hook for tests and provider refreshes, and avoid caller-level caches unless a caller has a documented freshness exception.
risks: A loader cache can return stale provider data until the TTL expires.
A global invalidation hook can become an accidental public API if it is not scoped clearly.
Callers with stricter freshness needs may need an opt-out.
follow_up_questions: How often can provider metadata change during one host session?
Do any callers need strongly fresh reads after a user-triggered sync?
Is there already a lifecycle event that can bust the loader cache?
assumptions: Most callers should share one freshness policy.
